channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cape Town dam levels recover after heavy rainfall, but water restrictions remain
item.description: Cape Town’s water storage system has experienced a sharp improvement following an unusual period of heavy rainfall, with dam levels rising from about 59.2% a year ago to approximately 70.8% as of mid-...

item.title: George Ruto Reacts Humorously to Online Speculation Linking Him to Biker Nairobi
item.description: George Kimutai Ruto, the youngest son of President William Ruto, has sparked online discussions after social media users linked him to the mysterious viral biker personality known as Brayo or Biker Na...

item.title: Enhanced Games CEO argues regulated doping competition could be safer than traditional sport ahead of Las Vegas debut
item.description: The chief executive of the controversial Enhanced Games has defended the upcoming Las Vegas event, arguing that a regulated environment for performance-enhancing drugs could ultimately be safer than c...

item.title: Armed suspect killed after shooting near White House security checkpoint
item.description: A shooting incident near the White House prompted a major security response after reports of gunfire were heard close to a Secret Service checkpoint on Saturday evening. Journalists gathered on the No...

item.title: 37 Ships Carrying Fuel, Food and Cargo Set to Arrive Lagos Ports
item.description: The Nigerian Ports Authority (NPA) has announced that a total of 37 vessels carrying petroleum products, food items, and various other consignments are expected to arrive at Lagos ports. According to ...

item.title: The 1970 Florence, Oregon Exploding Whale Disposal Attempt Using Dynamite
item.description: In November 1970, a large sperm whale measuring about 45 feet long and weighing approximately eight tons washed up dead on a beach near Florence, Oregon. At the time, Oregon’s beaches were considered ...

item.title: City of London mayor addresses AI-generated TikTok misinformation affecting London's reputation
item.description: Dame Susan Langley, the Lord Mayor of the City of London, has raised concerns about a wave of misleading and AI-generated TikTok content that she says is distorting perceptions of London and damaging ...

item.title: 32 individuals rescued after being stranded in a fast-rising river in Davao City
item.description: A total of 32 individuals were successfully rescued in Davao City after they became stranded in a raging river caused by heavy rainfall linked to a low pressure area (LPA). According to the report, su...

item.title: PM Shehbaz appreciates US-led diplomatic efforts as Pakistan supports ongoing Iran-US peace mediation
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Sunday appreciated United States President Donald Trump’s efforts towards promoting peace, describing them as “extraordinary” following a multi-nation phone call invol...

item.title: Couple Advised to Improve Communication and Openness About Their Sex Life
item.description: The article features a relationship advice exchange from Rappler’s ‘Two Pronged’ column, where a man named Ivan asks for guidance after learning that his girlfriend was not fully satisfied with their ...

item.title: U.S. pauses $14 billion Taiwan arms sale amid Middle East conflict and improving ties with China
item.description: A major U.S. arms sale to Taiwan valued at approximately $14 billion has been placed on hold, according to a senior U.S. military official, raising questions about the future of Washington’s security ...

item.title: Former Toronto Councillor and TTC Chair Howard Moscoe Dies at 86
item.description: Howard Moscoe, a well-known figure in Toronto municipal politics and former chair of the Toronto Transit Commission (TTC), has died at the age of 86. His passing was reported on May 23, 2026, promptin...

item.title: Explosion reported near railway track in Quetta; police and rescue teams respond
item.description: A loud explosion was reported in Quetta on Sunday, according to police officials. The incident occurred near a railway track within the city, prompting an immediate response from law enforcement and r...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk says Ladakh constitutional safeguard talks are ongoing after Centre meeting
item.description: Environmentalist and social reformer Sonam Wangchuk has said that discussions regarding constitutional safeguards for Ladakh under Article 371 are still ongoing and should be seen as a gradual process...

item.title: Automaker recalls nearly 17,000 vehicles in Canada over potential crash risk
item.description: An automaker has issued a recall affecting nearly 17,000 vehicles in Canada because of a safety-related defect that could increase the risk of a crash. The CTV News report states that the recall was a...

item.title: Alberta referendum question on potential separation from Canada revealed
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has announced a referendum question that could open the door to the province beginning the legal process for possible separation from Canada, intensifying long-standing ...

item.title: NSW launches Migrant Workers Centre to support temporary visa holders facing exploitation and underpayment
item.description: Temporary visa holders in New South Wales now have access to a dedicated Migrant Workers Centre designed to support those experiencing workplace exploitation, unsafe conditions and underpayment. The i...

item.title: UK set for early summer heatwave as temperatures approach May records
item.description: A spell of unusually hot weather is affecting the United Kingdom, with meteorologists indicating that a heatwave is likely to be officially declared in parts of the country as temperatures continue to...

item.title: US immigration policy change requires Green Card applicants to complete processing outside the United States, affecting Nigerian migrants
item.description: The United States government under President Donald Trump has introduced a major change to its immigration system that affects how foreign nationals apply for permanent residency. According to the dir...

item.title: Remembering publisher Krishan Chopra and his lasting impact on Indian publishing
item.description: This tribute article reflects on the life and legacy of Krishan Chopra, a veteran Indian publisher and editor who worked across major publishing houses including Penguin India, HarperCollins India, an...
